title: IPv4 header
summary: The IPv4 datagram header is at least 20 bytes: version/IHL, DSCP/ECN, total length, identification/flags/fragment-offset for fragmentation, TTL, protocol, header checksum, and the 32-bit source and destination addresses, followed by optional options. The IHL field gives the header length in 32-bit words.
source_version: RFC 791 (Internet Protocol), Section 3.1 Internet Header Format

ext.summary: Minimum 20-byte header. Carries addressing, fragmentation, a TTL hop limit, a protocol selector pointing at the next header (6 = TCP, 17 = UDP, 1 = ICMP), and a header-only checksum.

structure.meaning: Internet Header Length in 32-bit words (5 = 20 bytes minimum, up to 15 = 60 bytes).
Differentiated Services Code Point (QoS class); originally the Type of Service field.
Entire datagram size (header + data) in bytes, up to 65535.
Fragment group id, used to reassemble fragments of one datagram.
Bit 0 reserved (0), DF (Don't Fragment), MF (More Fragments).
Position of this fragment in the original datagram, in 8-byte units.
Hop limit; decremented by each router, datagram dropped at 0.
Next-layer protocol (per IANA): 1 ICMP, 6 TCP, 17 UDP, 41 IPv6-in-IPv4.
Ones-complement checksum over the header only; recomputed at each hop (TTL changes).
Optional (record route, timestamp, etc.), padded to a 32-bit boundary; present only when IHL > 5.

ext.example_decoded: First byte 0x45 = version 4, IHL 5 (20-byte header, no options). 0x00 = DSCP/ECN 0. 0x003C = total length 60 bytes.
notes: The Protocol field is the demultiplexer to the next header; values are the IANA 'Protocol Numbers' registry.
The header checksum covers only the header, so it must be recomputed whenever the TTL is decremented.
IPv6 dropped the header checksum and in-network fragmentation, simplifying its fixed 40-byte header.
